document.name: Morzsák Bar
document.shortDescription: Neighborhood bar by Móricz körtér — DJs, pálinka, and late nights on Bartók Béla út.
document.longDescription:
Morzsák Bar on Bartók Béla út is a local late spot between Móricz Zsigmond körtér and Gellért Hill with DJ weekends and creative cocktails. The room mixes students and Buda families. Events on morzsakbar.hu.

Sources: https://www.morzsakbar.hu/

document.name: Rudas Party Nights
document.shortDescription: Night spa parties at Rudas — DJ sets and pools in the Gellért Baths area foothills.
document.longDescription:
Rudas Bath night sessions combine thermal bathing with DJs and bars overlooking the Danube from the Gellért Baths area. Saturday night spa parties are a Budapest bucket-list experience. Tickets on rudasfurdo.hu.

Sources: https://www.rudasfurdo.hu/en/party

document.name: BinX Rooftop
document.shortDescription: Infopark rooftop parties — skyline cocktails and DJs above the Danube offices.
document.longDescription:
BinX Rooftop on Infopark sétány hosts summer DJ parties with Pest-Buda skyline views from office towers. After-work crowds mix with weekend dance nights. Guest list on binxrooftop.hu.

Sources: https://www.binxrooftop.hu/

document.name: Golyó Kávé
document.shortDescription: Evening bar nights at Golyó — craft beer and DJs by Kosztolányi tér on Bartók Béla út.
document.longDescription:
Golyó Kávé shifts from daytime coffee to beer and DJ nights on Bartók Béla út near Kosztolányi Dezső tér. The garden fills with locals on warm weekends. Program on golyokave.hu.

Sources: https://www.golyokave.hu/

document.name: Korona Club
document.shortDescription: Suburban club by Bikás park — house and retro nights on Fehérvári út in Újbuda.
document.longDescription:
Korona Club on Fehérvári út draws south-Buda crowds for weekend DJ nights and themed retro parties near Bikás park. Multiple rooms and late hours mirror Pest mall-club culture locally. Info on koronaclub.hu.

Sources: https://www.koronaclub.hu/

document.name: Gerbeaud
document.shortDescription: Landmark café and restaurant on Vörösmarty tér — pastries, Hungarian classics, and terrace views at the top of Váci utca.
document.longDescription:
Gerbeaud has anchored Vörösmarty tér since the 1850s with ornate salons, cakes, and full restaurant service. The ground-floor café and upstairs dining rooms suit breakfast through late dessert. Book ahead for holidays on gerbeaud.hu.

Sources: https://www.gerbeaud.hu/en

document.name: Bock Bistro
document.shortDescription: Wine-forward bistro near Deák — Bock cellar labels, seasonal Hungarian plates, and a cozy brick dining room.
document.longDescription:
Bock Bistro on Kért utca showcases the Bock family's wines alongside modern Hungarian cooking a short walk from Deák tér. The cellar list and daily menus are published on bockbistro.hu. Reservations recommended on weekends.

Sources: https://www.bockbistro.hu/

document.name: Kiosk Budapest
document.shortDescription: All-day bistro on the Danube bank — Mediterranean plates and terrace views toward Parliament.
document.longDescription:
Kiosk Budapest sits at Március 15. tér with a broad terrace overlooking the Danube and Parliament spires. The menu spans brunch through late dinner with a strong cocktail list. Reserve via kioskbudapest.com for prime riverside tables.

Sources: https://www.kioskbudapest.com/

document.name: Spoon Restaurant
document.shortDescription: Fine dining on a Danube ship — modern European menu and panoramic windows along the promenade.
document.longDescription:
Spoon's main restaurant deck serves contemporary European cuisine with full-length river views beside the Pest promenade. Lunch and dinner service include tasting menus and wine pairings. See spoon.hu for the current carte and mooring location.

Sources: https://www.spoon.hu/en/restaurant

document.name: Aszu Étterem
document.shortDescription: Fine dining at the foot of Andrássy út — Hungarian wine pairings and contemporary plates near Oktogon.
document.longDescription:
Aszu Étterem on Andrássy út 2 pairs a deep Hungarian wine list with refined seasonal cooking steps from Heroes' Square. The dining rooms suit business dinners and weekend tasting menus. Reserve via aszuetterem.hu.

Sources: https://www.aszuetterem.hu/

document.name: Arany Kaviár
document.shortDescription: Russian-Hungarian fine dining near the Opera — caviar, vodka pairings, and velvet salons on Október 6. utca.
document.longDescription:
Arany Kaviár on Október 6. utca serves Russian and Central European haute cuisine with an extensive vodka and wine cellar near Andrássy cultural venues. Tasting menus and private rooms suit special occasions. Reservations via aranykaviar.hu.

Sources: https://www.aranykaviar.hu/

document.name: Két Szerecsen
document.shortDescription: Classic Pest brasserie by Oktogon — Hungarian comfort food, terrace, and late kitchen on Nagymező utca.
document.longDescription:

document.name: Menza

document.name: Frici Papa
document.shortDescription: Beloved Jewish Quarter eatery — hearty Hungarian plates, daily menus, and no-frills dining room.
document.longDescription:

hu.shortDescription: Historic bistro in the Castle District — Hungarian classics and courtyard seating on Fortuna utca.
hu.longDescription:
Pest-Buda on Fortuna utca is a landmark Castle District restaurant with medieval cellar rooms and a leafy courtyard. Traditional Hungarian dishes and wine service suit tourists and locals alike. Reserve on pestbuda.hu.

Sources: https://www.pestbuda.hu/

document.name: Citadella Restaurant
document.shortDescription: Panoramic restaurant on Gellért Hill — Hungarian-European menu with terrace views from the Citadella.
document.longDescription:
Citadella Restaurant atop Gellért Hill serves lunch and dinner with sweeping Pest panoramas. Terrace tables fill at sunset for tourists hiking from the Liberty Statue. Menu on citadellarestaurant.hu.

Sources: https://www.citadellarestaurant.hu/

document.name: Remiz Restaurant
document.address: 1013 Budapest, Apáczai Csere János utca 1, Hungary
document.shortDescription: Danube-view restaurant in Tabán — Hungarian game dishes and terrace tables on the Buda bank.
document.longDescription:
Remiz Restaurant shares the riverside promenade in Tabán with panoramic Parliament views. The kitchen highlights Hungarian game and fish with an extensive wine cellar. Reservations via remizrestaurant.hu.

Sources: https://www.remizrestaurant.hu/
